name: N-260 Puigcerda Junction
summary: Most directly supported Puigcerda candidate because the official time schedule names N-260 Puigcerda at the route junction.
whyItMatters: This matches the itinerary's Puigcerda plan and uses an official schedule point before the race crosses into France.

access.estimated_route_from_base: From Puigcerda base, walk to the N-260 route area before closures; exact hotel/base location not assumed.
access.parking_transit_possibilities: If already based in Puigcerda, walking is preferable. Parking near the route is unknown and may be prohibited.
access.walk_bike_approach_notes: Walking from town is the safest assumption; do not assume vehicles can cross or use the N-260 after closure.
access.road_closure_notes: Unknown; needs Ajuntament/Servei Catala de Transit or Tour local closure notice.

pros: Official Tour time schedule explicitly names N-260 Puigcerda.
Likely easier logistics if lodging is in Puigcerda compared with driving to an isolated mountain point.
Town setting improves food/toilet fallback compared with rural roadside.
cons: Junction areas can be barrier-heavy and may offer poor public standing angles.
Traffic closures on the N-260 could isolate one side of town.
Riders may be moving quickly compared with later climbs.

logistics.parking: Unknown; do not rely on N-260 roadside or junction parking.
logistics.transit: Puigcerda has a rail station on the R3 line, but its usefulness for this trip/base and race-day changes are unknown.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Needs official Spanish/Catalan municipal traffic restrictions for Stage 3.

crowdRisk: medium: official town route point and convenient base, but less iconic than a summit finish.
bathroomFoodProximity: Likely better than isolated mountain roads because it is in Puigcerda, but specific accessible toilets/food after closures are unknown.
escapeRoute: Walk back into Puigcerda after the race convoy clears, staying on the same side of the route unless an official crossing is open.
safetyAssessment: Avoid roundabout islands and junction medians. Use only sidewalks or official spectator zones away from turning vehicles and race convoy lines.

timing.best_arrival_window: At least 3 hours before passage, earlier if official closures begin before the caravan.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium; route point is well-supported but local access is not yet sourced.

sources.title: Tour de France 2026 Stage 3 official page
sources.note: Supports Stage 3 route, mountain classification, official N-260 Puigcerda schedule point, and passage-time estimates.

sources.title: TDF trip data official route note
sources.note: Official ASO ArcGIS layer referenced by the app for Stage 3 route geometry; coordinate matches the app's Puigcerda route point.

sources.title: Puigcerda railway station background
sources.note: Supports the existence/location context of Puigcerda rail access; does not prove race-day rail plans.

open_questions: Which exact sidewalks or plazas near the N-260/N-152/N-154 junction are public spectator areas?
What roads close in Puigcerda, and when do pedestrian crossings stop?
Are any official spectator toilets, water points, or fan zones planned in Puigcerda?
